questions.q: Explain the concept of Intellectual Property Rights (IPRs) and its significance in the digital age. Discuss the protection of IPRs in cyberspace. (5 marks)
questions.answer: Intellectual Property Rights (IPRs) refer to the exclusive rights granted to creators of original works, such as music, literature, art, and inventions. The significance of IPRs lies in their ability to foster innovation and creativity by providing economic incentives for creators. In the digital age, IPRs are crucial in protecting the rights of creators from piracy and unauthorized use. Protection of IPRs in cyberspace involves enforcing laws and regulations that prevent copyright infringement, trademark counterfeiting, and patent infringement.
marking_scheme: Definition of IPRs (1 mark)
Significance of IPRs in the digital age (2 marks)
Protection of IPRs in cyberspace (2 marks)

questions.q: Explain the concept of copyright infringement and trademark counterfeiting in the digital age. Discuss the measures to prevent these crimes. (5 marks)
questions.answer: Copyright infringement and trademark counterfeiting are serious crimes in the digital age. Copyright infringement involves the unauthorized use or reproduction of copyrighted material, while trademark counterfeiting involves the sale of fake or counterfeit products. Measures to prevent these crimes include enforcing laws and regulations, using digital watermarking, and promoting awareness among consumers.
marking_scheme: Definition of copyright infringement and trademark counterfeiting (1 mark)
Consequences of copyright infringement and trademark counterfeiting (1 mark)
Measures to prevent copyright infringement (1 mark)
Measures to prevent trademark counterfeiting (1 mark)

questions.q: A company has developed a new software that uses a unique algorithm to provide a service. The software has a copyright symbol displayed on the website. If someone copies the software and sells it on the internet, what IPRs are being infringed? (5 marks)
questions.answer: The IPR being infringed is the copyright of the software. The software has a copyright symbol displayed on the website, indicating that the creator has exclusive rights over the software.
marking_scheme: Identification of the IPR being infringed (2 marks)
Explanation of the copyright symbol and its significance (2 marks)
Consequences of copyright infringement (1 mark)
